commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi Colt Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi Colt's most common replacement enquiries centre on the 1.1L 3A91 timing chain, the 1.5 DI-D OM639 injectors, and head gasket issues on higher-mileage 1.3L 4A90 units. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Colt 1.1 Timing Chain Failure - Why 3A91 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner wear allows chain slack, causing rattle and potential jump, leading to valve timing loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chain, tensioner, guides replaced before catastrophic failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early; 40k+ miles extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt 3A91 unit with upgraded tensioner, bench-tested, warranty included
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for engines with existing damage; 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: If your Colt 1.1 is rattling but still runs, a preventative chain kit from a specialist offers the best value. If the chain has jumped or valves are bent, a rebuilt engine at £395-£895 is far more economical than a dealer repair.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 3A91 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Colt

problems.h4: Colt 1.5 DI-D Injector Failure - OM639 Common Rail Issues
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Colt 1.5 DI-D, CZ3 DI-D (2004-2012, OM639.939)
problems.rootCause: High-pressure injector seals degrade, causing fuel leakage, rough running, and potential turbo damage from unburnt diesel.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: One or two injectors replaced, coding, pressure test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if turbo/DPF intact; 30k-50k miles extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt OM639 with upgraded injectors, turbo inspection, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for engines with multiple failures; 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: On a Colt DI-D worth £1,500-£3,000, replacing a single failed injector makes sense if the turbo is healthy. If multiple injectors or the turbo are suspect, a rebuilt OM639 at £495-£1,450 avoids repeated repair bills.
problems.cta: Get quotes for OM639 Mitsubishi Colt diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Colt 1.3 Head Gasket Failure - 4A90 Coolant Loss Issues
problems.rootCause: Cylinder head warping or gasket degradation allows coolant into combustion chambers, causing overheating and misfires.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Head removed, skimmed, new gasket fitted, pressure tested
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block is undamaged; 40k+ miles extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt 4A90 with upgraded gasket set, bench-tested, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for engines with block damage or high mileage; 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: If your Colt 1.3 has just started overheating with no knock, a head gasket repair from a specialist is cost-effective. If there's white smoke, oil contamination, or the engine knocks, a rebuilt 4A90 at £450-£995 is the safer long-term solution.

problems.h4: Colt LPG Conversion Valve Damage - 1.1L Burnt Valves
problems.rootCause: Lean LPG mixture and inadequate valve seat hardening cause exhaust valve recession, leading to compression loss and misfires.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head removed, valves recut or replaced, seats hardened
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if block/pistons undamaged; limited longevity on LPG

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt 3A91 with hardened seats, LPG-compatible setup, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for continued LPG use or conversion back to petrol; 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: LPG-related valve damage is rarely worth repairing on a budget Colt. A rebuilt 3A91 at £395-£895 with hardened seats is more reliable and often cheaper than repeated head work.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt Mitsubishi Colt 1.1 engines with LPG-ready specs

problems.h4: Colt 1.5 Turbo Failure - OM639 Actuator & Seal Issues
problems.rootCause: Turbo actuator sticks or oil seals fail, causing boost loss, blue smoke, or catastrophic turbo seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New or reconditioned turbo fitted, actuator calibrated, oil lines checked
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine internals clean; 40k+ miles extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt OM639 with inspected turbo, upgraded seals, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for engines with oil contamination or high mileage; 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: If your Colt DI-D has boost loss but no smoke or knock, a specialist turbo replacement is sensible. If there's blue smoke, oil in the intercooler, or metallic noise, a rebuilt OM639 at £495-£1,450 avoids further damage.
problems.cta: Get quotes for OM639 Mitsubishi Colt turbo diesel engines

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi Colt
finalCta.paragraph: Whether it's timing chain rattle, injector failure, or a blown head gasket, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mitsubishi Colt specialists. Every rebuilt or reconditioned engine includes upgraded components to address the original failure point, plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi Colt Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi Colt and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mitsubishi Colt Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Mitsubishi Colt is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Mitsubishi Colt 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L DI-D diesel, powered by the Mercedes-sourced OM639 engine, was the sole diesel option for the Mk6 Colt (2004-2012) and remains the most searched diesel replacement due to injector and turbo failure patterns at higher mileage.
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Colt 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Mitsubishi Colt 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The entry-level 1.0L 3-cylinder petrol unit powered early Mk5 Colts (1996-2000); simple and economical but now rare in the replacement market due to age and lower survival rates.
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Colt 1.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Colt 1.1L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.1L 3-cylinder 3A91 engine was the volume petrol engine for the Mk6 Colt (2004-2012); lightweight and economical but notorious for timing chain tensioner wear causing rattle and potential valve damage.
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Colt 1.1L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Colt 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.3L 4-cylinder engines (4G13 in Mk5, 4A90 in Mk6) were the Colt's most versatile petrol units; reliable when maintained but prone to head gasket failure on higher-mileage examples.
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Colt 1.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Colt 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.5L petrol engines (4G15 in Mk5, 4A91 in Mk6) offered more torque for heavier loads; robust designs but susceptible to oil sludge (4G15) or timing chain guide wear (4A91) if servicing is neglected.
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Colt 1.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Colt 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L 4G92 MIVEC engine powered the iconic Colt GTI (1992-1996); a high-revving performance unit sought by enthusiasts but requiring careful maintenance to avoid head gasket and bearing wear.
commonFailurePoints: crankshaft bearing wear on tuned engines
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Colt 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Colt 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8L 4G93 engine was the larger GTI option; rare in the UK but valued by restorers for its strong mid-range torque, though oil consumption and cooling issues are common on high-mileage examples.
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Colt 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Colt 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0L 6G72 V6 powered the Colt Rodeo SUV variant (1998-2004); a smooth, torquey engine but prone to sensor failures and oil leaks if maintenance intervals are exceeded.
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Colt 3.0L petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Mitsubishi Colt Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Mitsubishi Colt's UK production history, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The iconic GTI models arrived with high-revving MIVEC petrol engines, creating a lasting enthusiast following and specialist replacement demand.
keyChanges: MIVEC variable valve timing on 4G92 engine
knownFor: 4G92 MIVEC is a sought-after enthusiast engine
Head gasket weakness on high-mileage GTIs
Simple, robust design makes rebuilds affordable
Strong aftermarket support for performance parts
years.cta: Get quotes for 1992-1996 Mitsubishi Colt GTI engines
years.ticker: Colt GTI engine rebuild • 4G92 head gasket replacement • Colt 1.6 MIVEC specialist rebuild

years.description: The Mk5 Colt expanded to 5-door practicality while the Rodeo SUV variant introduced the 3.0L V6, broadening the model's appeal and engine replacement scope.
keyChanges: New platform with 3-door and 5-door body options
Colt Rodeo SUV variant launched with V6 power
knownFor: 4G13/4G15 are robust if serviced regularly
Oil sludge issues on neglected 4G15 units
Rodeo V6 sensor failures common at 100k+ miles
Affordable rebuilt prices make repair economical
years.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Colt Mk5 engine prices
years.ticker: Colt 1.3 engine replacement • Rodeo V6 crank sensor fix • Colt 1.5 oil sludge repair

years.description: The best-selling Mk6 Colt introduced the 3A91 1.1L, 4A90 1.3L and OM639 1.5 DI-D engines, with timing chain and injector failures now driving most replacement enquiries.
keyChanges: All-new Z3A platform with modern styling
Mercedes-sourced OM639 1.5 DI-D diesel option added
knownFor: 3A91 timing chain rattle (50k-80k miles) is the #1 failure
OM639 injector failure drives diesel replacements
4A90 head gasket issues on high-mileage examples
Highest UK search volume for Colt engine replacement
years.cta: Get quotes for 2004-2012 Mitsubishi Colt replacement engines
years.ticker: Colt 1.1 timing chain fix • OM639 injector replacement • Colt 1.3 head gasket cost

years.preview: Present - End of Production & Legacy Support
years.description: Production ended in 2012 but strong parts availability and affordable rebuilt engines keep Mk6 Colts on UK roads, with replacement demand focused on 3A91 and OM639 units.
keyChanges: No direct successor in UK market
Growing "repair vs replace" decisions as cars age
knownFor: Excellent value rebuilt engines (£395-£1,450 typical)
Strong breaker network for used units
Supply & fit services widely available
Ideal candidate for cost-effective engine replacement
years.cta: Find Mitsubishi Colt legacy engine replacement deals

faq.h2: Mitsubishi Colt Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mitsubishi Colt engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Mitsubishi Colt engine typically costs £395-£1,450 supply-only, depending on engine code and failure severity. Supply & fit adds £350-£650 labour. Used units start from £295 but carry shorter warranties.
keyPoints: 1.1L 3A91 rebuilt: £395-£895 typical
1.5 DI-D OM639 rebuilt: £495-£1,450 typical
Labour for supply & fit: £350-£650 additional
All EngineMarket quotes include 12-month warranty minimum
items.cta: Compare live Mitsubishi Colt engine replacement prices now

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Mitsubishi Colt engine or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: For most Colts valued £1,000-£3,000, a rebuilt engine at £395-£1,450 is far more economical than scrapping (£150-£400) or buying another used car with unknown history.
keyPoints: Rebuilt Colt engines cost less than many used cars
12-month warranty provides peace of mind vs unknown used car
Supply & fit available if you lack workshop access
Keep a known-history car vs gamble on another
items.cta: Get a tailored quote for your Mitsubishi Colt today

items.question: What is the most common Mitsubishi Colt engine problem?
items.answer: The 1.1L 3A91 petrol engine's timing chain tensioner failure is the most searched Colt engine issue, causing rattle and potential valve damage between 50k-80k miles.
keyPoints: 3A91 timing chain rattle is the #1 Colt engine query
OM639 diesel injector failure is second most common
4A90 1.3L head gasket issues affect higher-mileage examples
All three have affordable rebuilt replacement options
items.cta: Find rebuilt 3A91 engines for your Mitsubishi Colt

items.question: Which Mitsubishi Colt engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The 1.3L 4A90 petrol engine has the best reliability reputation among Colt units, with fewer catastrophic failures than the 1.1L 3A91 or 1.5 DI-D OM639 when properly maintained.
keyPoints: 4A90 1.3L has robust design and good parts availability
Regular oil changes prevent most 4A90 issues
Still prone to head gasket failure at 80k+ miles
Rebuilt 4A90 units cost £450-£995 typical
items.cta: Compare 4A90 Mitsubishi Colt engine prices

items.question: Can I fit a different engine in my Mitsubishi Colt?
items.answer: While enthusiast swaps like 4G63 exist, most UK Colt owners benefit from direct-fit rebuilt engines (3A91, 4A90, OM639) that require no ECU or wiring modifications.
keyPoints: Direct-fit rebuilt engines avoid complex conversions
Same engine code ensures ECU compatibility
Supply & fit specialists handle all coding if needed
Performance swaps require specialist fabrication
items.cta: Get quotes for direct-fit Mitsubishi Colt engines

items.question: How long does a rebuilt Mitsubishi Colt engine last?
items.answer: A properly rebuilt Colt engine from an EngineMarket specialist typically lasts 40,000-80,000+ miles with regular servicing, backed by a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.
keyPoints: Rebuilt units include upgraded failure-prone components
12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard
Regular oil changes critical for longevity
Many rebuilt Colts exceed 100k miles post-rebuild
items.cta: Find rebuilt Colt engines with warranty coverage

items.question: Do Mitsubishi Colt used engines come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes - used Colt engines sourced through EngineMarket suppliers typically include 30-90 day warranties, with rebuilt/reconditioned units carrying 12-month unlimited mileage coverage.
keyPoints: Used engine warranties: 30-90 days typical
Rebuilt/recon engines: 12-month minimum standard
Warranty terms vary by supplier - confirm before ordering
All EngineMarket suppliers are vetted UK specialists
items.cta: Check used Mitsubishi Colt engine warranty options

items.question: How do I find my Mitsubishi Colt engine code?
items.answer: Your Colt's engine code is stamped on the engine block (visible near the gearbox) and listed on your V5C logbook under "engine number" or "fuel type" sections.
keyPoints: Common codes: 3A91 (1.1L), 4A90 (1.3L), OM639 (1.5 DI-D)
V5C logbook lists engine number/code
Enter reg on EngineMarket for instant DVLA verification
Specialists can confirm code from photos if unsure
items.cta: Identify your Mitsubishi Colt engine code instantly

items.question: Is supply & fit available for Mitsubishi Colt engines?
items.answer: Yes - EngineMarket's network includes UK specialists offering full supply & fit for all Colt engine types, with labour typically £350-£650 added to the engine price.
keyPoints: Supply & fit available nationwide for Colt models
Labour cost: £350-£650 typical depending on location
Includes removal, fitting, coding, fluids and road test
Single warranty covers both engine and installation
items.cta: Find Mitsubishi Colt supply & fit deals near you

items.question: What's the difference between reconditioned and rebuilt Colt engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned Colt engines have worn parts replaced after cleaning/inspection; rebuilt units undergo full strip-down with upgraded components addressing known failure points like timing chains.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: cleaned, inspected, worn parts replaced
Rebuilt: full strip-down, upgraded failure-prone components
Rebuilt typically costs slightly more but offers longer life
Both include minimum 12-month warranty via EngineMarket
items.cta: Compare reconditioned vs rebuilt Mitsubishi Colt engines
